meta_title: Removals & Storage FAQs | Mark Ratcliffe Moving Eastbourne
meta_description: Common questions about Eastbourne removals, storage, packing and international moves. Honest answers from a family-run BAR-registered mover.
intro_paragraph: These are the questions we get asked most often by Eastbourne, Hailsham and Sussex customers. If your question is not here, give us a call on 01323 848 008 or email mark@markratcliffemoving.co.uk — we are happy to help.

hero.kicker: Honest answers from a family-run mover

sections.body_html: These are the questions we get asked most often by Eastbourne, Hailsham and Sussex customers. If your question is not here, give us a call on 01323 848 008 or email mark@markratcliffemoving.co.uk — we are happy to help.

sections.heading: Booking & quotes
sections.body_html:
Booking & quotes

How do I get a removal quote?

Three ways: phone us on 01323 848 008, complete our online quote form, or request a free in-home or video survey. For accurate pricing we recommend the survey — it takes 30-45 minutes.

Is the quote binding?

Yes. Once we have surveyed your home and issued a written quote, that price holds unless your inventory changes significantly. There are no hidden surcharges or fuel add-ons.

How long is a quote valid for?

30 days from the date issued. If your move date is more than 30 days out, we can reissue or extend the quote.

What deposit do you require?

20% of the quoted price secures your move date. Balance is payable on the day of completion.

Do you charge for the survey?

No — all surveys are free with absolutely no obligation.

sections.heading: On move day
sections.body_html:
On move day

What time will the crew arrive?

Typically 8:00–8:30am for a full day move. We will confirm a more precise window the day before.

Do I need to be there all day?

Not necessarily — but it helps to be there at both ends to direct the crew on where things go. Many customers leave once loading is complete and meet the lorry at the destination.

What if my move-day parking is restricted?

We arrange parking suspensions in advance for known restrictions. Mention this during the survey.

What happens if it rains?

We move in all weathers. Pad-wrapping protects furniture from rain damage between the house and the lorry. We have waterproof covers for the loading area.

Should I pack the day before?

For a full removal with our packing service — no, we pack on a pre-move day. For self-pack moves, yes — be packed the night before so the crew can load straight away.

sections.heading: Packing & protection
sections.body_html:
Packing & protection

Do you supply boxes?

Yes — included with the quoted packing service, or available to buy from our Lower Dicker packaging shop if you are self-packing.

Can I leave clothes in drawers?

For lightweight items in sturdy drawer units, yes. For heavy items or fragile drawers we ask you to empty them.

Do you pack electronics?

Yes. We are trained to handle TVs, computers and sound systems. Original boxes are best where you have them.

What does pad-wrap actually mean?

Every piece of furniture is individually wrapped in a thick quilted blanket inside your home, taped, labelled, then carried to the lorry. It is only unwrapped once it has been placed in its final position in your new home.

sections.heading: Storage
sections.body_html:
Storage

How are your storage rooms different from a self-storage warehouse?

Each customer has their own individual steel-walled room rather than a shelf or cage inside a shared warehouse. 24-hour CCTV, alarmed access.

What is the minimum storage term?

Four weeks. After that you pay month-by-month with no long contract and no early-exit fees.

Can I access my storage room?

Yes — by appointment during depot hours, normally with 24-hour notice.

Is my stored property insured?

Storage insurance is available as an add-on and strongly recommended. We arrange cover through our broker.

sections.heading: International
sections.body_html:
International

How long does an international removal take?

Air freight to Europe is 5-10 days door to door. Sea freight to Australia, USA or Thailand is 6-12 weeks. We give a more accurate window once we know destination and volume.

Do you handle customs paperwork?

Yes — inventory packing list, valued inventory, customs declarations and Transfer of Residence (ToR1) paperwork are all prepared by us.

Can you ship to Thailand?

Yes — UK to Thailand is one of our specialist routes. We ship regularly to Bangkok, Pattaya, Phuket and Chiang Mai.

What can't I ship internationally?

Most countries prohibit firearms, explosives, narcotics, certain plants and seeds, ivory, and some food items. Many restrict alcohol and electronics. We provide a destination-specific list.

sections.heading: Costs & payment
sections.body_html:
Costs & payment

How and when do I pay the balance?

Balance is due on the day of completion. We accept bank transfer (faster payments), card or cheque.

What if my move date changes?

No charge for postponements, cancellations or key waits. We simply update the diary at no extra cost.

Are tips for the crew expected?

Tips are entirely optional and never expected. If your crew has gone above and beyond, a tip is appreciated — many customers offer £10-20 per person at the end of a good move.

Do you offer payment plans?

For larger or international moves we can sometimes split the balance — speak to us during the quote process.

sections.heading: About Mark Ratcliffe Moving & Storage
sections.body_html:
About Mark Ratcliffe Moving & Storage

How long have you been trading?

Mark Ratcliffe Moving has been operating since 1982 — over forty years. We are family-run, based at Lower Dicker near Hailsham, and have moved tens of thousands of households across Sussex and beyond.

Are you really a BAR member?

Yes. We are a full member of the British Association of Removers Overseas Group. Members are independently inspected against standards covering premises, vehicles, training, customer service and complaints handling. Ask us for our membership number if you would like to verify it.

Do you employ your own crews or use contractors?

All our crews are directly employed, uniformed and trained in-house at our staff training centre to BS 8564-aligned standards. We do not use agency or zero-hours labour. This matters because the people moving your possessions care about the company's reputation and the quality of the work.

sections.heading: Insurance & protection
sections.body_html:
Insurance & protection

What insurance is included with my removal?

Standard goods-in-transit insurance is included on every quote — currently up to £40,000 per load. Higher-value declared cover is available as an add-on. We can also recommend specialist insurers for art, antiques and high-value collections.

What if something is damaged during the move?

You raise a claim within seven days of the move, supplying photographs and the item details. We aim to settle straightforward claims within 14 days. Our breakage rate is extremely low — typically under 1 in 50 moves has any claim at all.

Are you BAR Advance Payment Guarantee protected?

Yes. As a BAR member we participate in the Advance Payment Guarantee scheme, which protects deposits up to a specified limit in the unlikely event of company failure between deposit and move date.

raw_body:
Home › FAQs
Honest answers from a family-run mover

Frequently Asked Questions

Get a Free Quote Call 01323 848 008

These are the questions we get asked most often by Eastbourne, Hailsham and Sussex customers. If your question is not here, give us a call on 01323 848 008 or email mark@markratcliffemoving.co.uk — we are happy to help.

Booking & quotes

How do I get a removal quote?

Three ways: phone us on 01323 848 008, complete our online quote form, or request a free in-home or video survey. For accurate pricing we recommend the survey — it takes 30-45 minutes.

Is the quote binding?

Yes. Once we have surveyed your home and issued a written quote, that price holds unless your inventory changes significantly. There are no hidden surcharges or fuel add-ons.

How long is a quote valid for?

30 days from the date issued. If your move date is more than 30 days out, we can reissue or extend the quote.

What deposit do you require?

20% of the quoted price secures your move date. Balance is payable on the day of completion.

Do you charge for the survey?

No — all surveys are free with absolutely no obligation.

On move day

What time will the crew arrive?

Typically 8:00–8:30am for a full day move. We will confirm a more precise window the day before.

Do I need to be there all day?

Not necessarily — but it helps to be there at both ends to direct the crew on where things go. Many customers leave once loading is complete and meet the lorry at the destination.

What if my move-day parking is restricted?

We arrange parking suspensions in advance for known restrictions. Mention this during the survey.

What happens if it rains?

We move in all weathers. Pad-wrapping protects furniture from rain damage between the house and the lorry. We have waterproof covers for the loading area.

Should I pack the day before?

For a full removal with our packing service — no, we pack on a pre-move day. For self-pack moves, yes — be packed the night before so the crew can load straight away.

Packing & protection

Do you supply boxes?

Yes — included with the quoted packing service, or available to buy from our Lower Dicker packaging shop if you are self-packing.

For lightweight items in sturdy drawer units, yes. For heavy items or fragile drawers we ask you to empty them.

Do you pack electronics?

Yes. We are trained to handle TVs, computers and sound systems. Original boxes are best where you have them.

What does pad-wrap actually mean?

Every piece of furniture is individually wrapped in a thick quilted blanket inside your home, taped, labelled, then carried to the lorry. It is only unwrapped once it has been placed in its final position in your new home.

Storage

How are your storage rooms different from a self-storage warehouse?

Each customer has their own individual steel-walled room rather than a shelf or cage inside a shared warehouse. 24-hour CCTV, alarmed access.

What is the minimum storage term?

Four weeks. After that you pay month-by-month with no long contract and no early-exit fees.

Can I access my storage room?

Yes — by appointment during depot hours, normally with 24-hour notice.

Is my stored property insured?

Storage insurance is available as an add-on and strongly recommended. We arrange cover through our broker.

International

How long does an international removal take?

Air freight to Europe is 5-10 days door to door. Sea freight to Australia, USA or Thailand is 6-12 weeks. We give a more accurate window once we know destination and volume.

Do you handle customs paperwork?

Yes — inventory packing list, valued inventory, customs declarations and Transfer of Residence (ToR1) paperwork are all prepared by us.

Can you ship to Thailand?

Yes — UK to Thailand is one of our specialist routes. We ship regularly to Bangkok, Pattaya, Phuket and Chiang Mai.

What can't I ship internationally?

Most countries prohibit firearms, explosives, narcotics, certain plants and seeds, ivory, and some food items. Many restrict alcohol and electronics. We provide a destination-specific list.

Costs & payment

How and when do I pay the balance?

Balance is due on the day of completion. We accept bank transfer (faster payments), card or cheque.

What if my move date changes?

No charge for postponements, cancellations or key waits. We simply update the diary at no extra cost.

Are tips for the crew expected?

Tips are entirely optional and never expected. If your crew has gone above and beyond, a tip is appreciated — many customers offer £10-20 per person at the end of a good move.

Do you offer payment plans?

For larger or international moves we can sometimes split the balance — speak to us during the quote process.

About Mark Ratcliffe Moving & Storage

How long have you been trading?

Mark Ratcliffe Moving has been operating since 1982 — over forty years. We are family-run, based at Lower Dicker near Hailsham, and have moved tens of thousands of households across Sussex and beyond.

Are you really a BAR member?

Yes. We are a full member of the British Association of Removers Overseas Group. Members are independently inspected against standards covering premises, vehicles, training, customer service and complaints handling. Ask us for our membership number if you would like to verify it.

Do you employ your own crews or use contractors?

All our crews are directly employed, uniformed and trained in-house at our staff training centre to BS 8564-aligned standards. We do not use agency or zero-hours labour. This matters because the people moving your possessions care about the company's reputation and the quality of the work.

Insurance & protection

What insurance is included with my removal?

Standard goods-in-transit insurance is included on every quote — currently up to £40,000 per load. Higher-value declared cover is available as an add-on. We can also recommend specialist insurers for art, antiques and high-value collections.

What if something is damaged during the move?

You raise a claim within seven days of the move, supplying photographs and the item details. We aim to settle straightforward claims within 14 days. Our breakage rate is extremely low — typically under 1 in 50 moves has any claim at all.

Are you BAR Advance Payment Guarantee protected?

Yes. As a BAR member we participate in the Advance Payment Guarantee scheme, which protects deposits up to a specified limit in the unlikely event of company failure between deposit and move date.
